title: Comparison Operators Practice
instructions: Write functions using comparison operators to solve problems. Focus on proper operator usage and chaining comparisons.
hints: Use chained comparisons for range checks: min_val <= number <= max_val
Percentage should be between 0 and 100 inclusive

testCases.description: is_in_range should return True when number is within range
testCases.validation: assert is_in_range(5, 1, 10) == True, '5 should be in range 1-10'

testCases.description: is_in_range should return True when number equals boundary values
testCases.validation: assert is_in_range(1, 1, 10) == True and is_in_range(10, 1, 10) == True, 'Boundary values should be inclusive'

testCases.description: compare_strings should return correct comparison result
testCases.validation: assert compare_strings('apple', 'banana') == 'first' and compare_strings('cat', 'cat') == 'equal', 'Should compare strings lexicographically'

testCases.description: is_valid_percentage should return True for values 0-100
testCases.validation: assert is_valid_percentage(50) == True and is_valid_percentage(0) == True and is_valid_percentage(100) == True, 'Values 0-100 should be valid'

testCases.description: is_in_range should return False when number is outside range
testCases.validation: assert is_in_range(0, 1, 10) == False and is_in_range(11, 1, 10) == False, 'Numbers outside range should return False'

testCases.description: is_valid_percentage should reject negative values and values over 100
testCases.validation: assert is_valid_percentage(-1) == False and is_valid_percentage(101) == False, 'Values outside 0-100 should be invalid'

description: Write functions using Python comparison operators (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=) and chained comparisons. Practice range checking with inclusive boundaries, lexicographic string comparison, and percentage validation — building a solid foundation for conditional logic.
